_comment: Registry for the primary-model swap pool. Each entry describes a model that llama-router.ini knows how to load. The id matches the [section] name in llama-router.ini and the model id at the API level. model-swap.sh reads this to compose its yad popup, predict memory, and pick the larger-context model for compaction. Add an entry here when you add a model to llama-router.ini.

gpt-oss-120b.description: Frontier reasoning, 128K context, MoE expert offload to system RAM

_notes.vram_required_mb: Includes weights + KV cache + compute buffer. Measured live, not theoretical.
_notes.dram_required_mb: Host-side RAM the model needs for MoE expert offload. 0 for fully-GPU-resident models.
_notes.expected_load_seconds: Cold load from SSD. Used for the 'this will take ~N min' line in the popup.
_notes.expected_gen_tok_per_sec: Steady-state generation. Used to estimate re-eval time after a model swap.
_notes.compaction_agent: model-swap.sh picks whichever model has the highest context_tokens as the compaction agent. Today that is gpt-oss-120b. Pinned in opencode.json template via agent.compaction.model.
